title: Airlines Raise Fuel Surcharges as Middle East Crisis Drives Oil Prices Higher
title_cn: 中东危机推高油价，航空公司上调燃油附加费
content:
Airlines across Asia and Europe are significantly increasing fuel surcharges as the Middle East conflict pushes aviation fuel prices to record highs. Starting April 1, major carriers including Cathay Pacific and Greater Bay Airlines have announced substantial hikes in fuel-related fees passed on to passengers.

According to S&P Global energy data, global aviation fuel prices reached $197 per barrel in the week ending March 20, nearly double the $99.4 per barrel recorded just before the conflict began on February 27. This dramatic increase has forced airlines to adjust their pricing structures to offset rising operational costs.

Cathay Pacific, Hong Kong's flagship carrier, has implemented its second round of fuel surcharge increases in late March. The cumulative rise exceeds 170% across all route categories. Short-haul flights now carry a fuel surcharge of HK$389, up from the original HK$142—representing a 174% increase. Medium-haul routes have seen fees rise from HK$264 to HK$725, while long-haul flights now charge HK$1,560 compared to the previous HK$569.

Greater Bay Airlines has followed suit with similar increases. Flights from Hong Kong to the Maldives will see fuel surcharges jump 34% to HK$725 per segment, while other international destinations (excluding mainland China) will rise from HK$290 to HK$389 per segment. These increases come on top of previous hikes of 90% and 104% respectively.

The impact extends beyond Hong Kong. Vietnam Airlines announced it will suspend 23 domestic flights per week starting April 1 due to fuel supply concerns. Vietnam imports two-thirds of its aviation fuel, primarily from China and Thailand, and the conflict has disrupted these supply chains.

Industry analysts note that fuel typically accounts for approximately 30% of an airline's total operating costs. With global aviation industry profit margins averaging just 3.6% in 2025, the current fuel price surge poses a significant threat to airline profitability. The International Air Transport Association (IATA) has projected that airfares could rise by 8-9% if the situation persists.

Chinese mainland airlines are also preparing for adjustments. China Southern, Xiamen Air, Spring Airlines, and Juneyao Airlines have confirmed increases to fuel surcharges on select international routes. Domestic route adjustments are expected to take effect on April 5, when the next pricing window opens.

The crisis has prompted some governments to take emergency measures. Australia has released additional fuel supplies from its mandatory domestic reserves, while Cambodia has reduced import duties and value-added taxes on gasoline and diesel to zero to ease domestic price pressures.

For travelers, the increases mean higher ticket prices across the board. Many passengers are reportedly "stockpiling" tickets by booking flights in advance before further increases take effect. Travel platforms have reported surging demand for bookings two weeks to one month ahead of travel dates.

The situation highlights how geopolitical conflicts can have immediate and far-reaching effects on global commerce and everyday consumers. With no immediate resolution to the Middle East crisis in sight, airlines and passengers alike are bracing for continued volatility in fuel costs and airfares.
translation: 随着中东冲突将航空燃料价格推至历史新高，亚洲和欧洲的航空公司正在大幅上调燃油附加费。从4月1日起，包括国泰航空和大湾区航空在内的主要航空公司宣布大幅上调向乘客收取的燃油相关费用。
根据标普全球能源数据，在截至3月20日的一周内，全球航空燃油价格达到每桶197美元，几乎是2月27日冲突开始前每桶99.4美元的两倍。这一大幅增长迫使航空公司调整定价结构以抵消不断上升的运营成本。
香港旗舰航空公司国泰航空在3月下旬实施了第二轮燃油附加费上调。累计涨幅在所有航线类别中超过170%。短途航班的燃油附加费现为389港元，高于原来的142港元，涨幅达174%。中途航线的费用从264港元上涨至725港元，而长途航班现在收取1560港元，而此前为569港元。
大湾区航空也采取了类似的上调措施。从香港飞往马尔代夫的航班燃油附加费将上涨34%至每航段725港元，而其他国际目的地（不包括中国内地）将从每航段290港元上涨至389港元。这些涨幅是在之前分别上涨90%和104%的基础上追加的。
影响不仅限于香港。越南航空宣布，由于燃油供应问题，将从4月1日起每周暂停23个国内航班。越南三分之二的航空燃油依赖进口，主要来自中国和中国台湾，而冲突已经扰乱了这些供应链。
行业分析师指出，燃油通常占航空公司总运营成本的约30%。考虑到2025年全球航空业平均利润率仅为3.6%，当前的燃油价格飙升对航空公司的盈利能力构成重大威胁。国际航空运输协会（IATA）预测，如果局势持续，机票价格可能上涨8-9%。
中国内地航空公司也在准备调整。中国南方航空、厦门航空、春秋航空和吉祥航空已确认上调部分国际航线的燃油附加费。国内航线调整预计将在4月5日下一个定价窗口开启时生效。
这场危机促使一些政府采取紧急措施。澳大利亚已从强制性国内储备中释放额外的燃油供应，而柬埔寨已将汽油和柴油的进口关税和增值税降至零，以缓解国内价格压力。
对旅客来说，这些上涨意味着全面更高的机票价格。据报道，许多乘客正在"囤票"，在进一步上涨生效前提前预订航班。旅游平台报告称，提前两周到一个月预订的航班需求激增。
这种情况凸显了地缘政治冲突如何能够对全球商业和普通消费者产生即时而深远的影响。鉴于中东危机短期内看不到解决方案，航空公司和乘客都在为燃油成本和机票价格的持续波动做好准备。

vocabulary.example: The hotel added a 10% surcharge for weekend stays.
The company raised prices to offset rising costs.
The cumulative effect of the changes was significant.
The market experienced high volatility this week.
The region is of great geopolitical importance.
The airline decided to suspend flights to the region.
The storm disrupted power supplies across the city.
The company is focused on improving profitability.
